original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Insect bites and stings: antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE infectious-disease, allergy, travel, and referral adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: People with an insect bite or sting, including tick, exotic-insect, local reaction, infection, or systemic-reaction context.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Community and primary-care assessment, reassessment, and clinician-led referral.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for documenting bite or sting type, local inflammatory or allergic features, infection and systemic-reaction assessment, worsening, severe pain, immunocompromise, travel, and sensitive-site context. It does not generate treatment, antibiotics, antihistamines, referral, or urgency.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — assessment and advice
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting rapid-onset local reaction, redness, itch, type and severity, possible infection, systemic reaction, tick context, and worsening or systemic illness only when assessed.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.8–1.1.11 — reassessment, referral, and specialist-advice qualifiers
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting infection development, rapid worsening, systemic illness, severe disproportionate pain, previous systemic reaction, immunocompromise, mouth, throat or eye location, exotic exposure, fever, persistent lesions, and travel context.

title: Insect bites and stings: antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE infectious-disease, allergy, travel, and referral adaptation
population: People with an insect bite or sting, including tick, exotic-insect, local reaction, infection, or systemic-reaction context.
intended_setting: Community and primary-care assessment, reassessment, and clinician-led referral.
